framework: A path qualifies only when >=3 prominent, proven people in the field visibly share the capability, each with a resolving link — the pattern across standouts is the evidence, not market demand. Ranked by strength of that pattern. Curated editorial, not DOL-derived.

paths.path: Become an embedded chief/in-house economist inside a tech company
paths.what_it_is: Apply PhD-level econometrics, market design, and causal inference directly inside a tech company's product and policy decisions, often as a named Chief Economist or economics-team lead.
paths.why_it_signals: Multiple major tech companies have independently created and repeatedly filled a Chief Economist role with academically trained economists, showing this is a real, repeatable career track rather than one company's idiosyncratic hire.

paths.path: Publish a public-facing economics newsletter or blog with a real following
paths.what_it_is: Write regularly for a broad public audience (Substack, blog) translating economic analysis into accessible, widely-read commentary, building a personal platform alongside or instead of an academic post.
paths.why_it_signals: Several trained economists — from a Nobel laureate to tenured professors — have each built independent, large public readerships this way, showing the capability (not one-off virality) is what carries the following.

paths.path: Turn research into an open, policy-used public data tool
paths.what_it_is: Build and maintain a large-scale, publicly accessible dataset or interactive tool (mobility, inequality, tax data) that policymakers, journalists, and other researchers directly use.
paths.why_it_signals: Several economists working in overlapping teams have each shipped and sustained a named public data infrastructure project used well beyond academia, showing this is a buildable, repeatable capability rather than a single lucky project.
